reflection:
seven hours. you actually stayed in bed through the sweating instead of getting up, and you know exactly why, the l-theanine. that's two nights running now. that one belongs in the keep pile, it's working.

here's what i keep seeing in your own numbers. your head is in a genuinely good place. stress 1, anxiety 1, mood 7, gratitude flowing, you're excited about ping-pong and the GX10 box today. but your body is running hot and wired underneath all that calm. standing heart rate around 80, feverish score crept from 0 to 7 across four days, sweating in bed. you said it yourself, stress-wise you're good but your heart feels on fire and you don't know where it's coming from.

that disconnect is the whole story right now. it isn't anxiety, your mind already let go. so stop trying to think your way out of what is a body signal. you found two real things this week, l-theanine for sleep and the float pad for your hand, better than any cream. lean on what already works instead of hunting the next fix.

one thing for today. the night sweats are climbing, so take the heat out of the room before bed, lower the temp, lighter bedding. don't add new variables, just keep the l-theanine and cool things down.

now go play ping-pong and enjoy the box. you earned a good day.
opener: seven hours, mind calm but the body still running hot
focus_for_today: keep the l-theanine, cool the room before bed, the night sweats are climbing
